records.ety: [F. rgion, from L. regio a direction, a boundary line, region, fr. regere to guide, direct. See Regimen.]

defs: One of the grand districts or quarters into which any space or surface, as of the earth or the heavens, is conceived of as divided; hence, in general, a portion of space or territory of indefinite extent; country; province; district; tract.
quotes: If thence he 'scappe, into whatever world, Or unknown region.

defs: Tract, part, or space, lying about and including anything; neighborhood; vicinity; sphere.
quotes: Philip, tetrarch of .. the region of Trachonitis.

defs: The upper air; the sky; the heavens.
quotes: Anon the dreadful thunder Doth rend the region.
He is of too high a region.
